sentence_text: The ruling opened up new appeal options.
pair_rationale: Both objects are appeal-domain outcomes; options can become available, whereas deadlines are set, extended, or missed rather than opened up.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; revises opportunities/objections pair because objections could be caused or invited by a policy; legal appeal frame blocks disclosure/speaking readings.

sentence_text: The defendant got off with a warning.
intended_sense: receive no or only light punishment
pair_rationale: Both complements are legal-outcome nouns; a warning fits the lenient-penalty frame, whereas a conviction is the adverse legal outcome rather than something one gets off with.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces transparent leave-vehicle sense with legal leniency sense to improve C1 suitability and suppress transport/literal readings.

sentence_text: The researchers carried out the analysis.
pair_rationale: Both objects are research-domain nouns; analysis is a task that can be carried out, whereas hypothesis is a proposition to test.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Conduct/perform task sense is strong for academic LJT; use plausible wrong task objects.

sentence_text: The campaign brought in large expenses.
intended_sense: attract or generate income or support
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to campaign-finance discourse; donations are incoming support that can be brought in, whereas expenses are costs rather than generated income or support.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces consultant/consultation role contrast with finance-support sense for stronger C1-level semantic dependency.

sentence_text: The gallery put on a retrospective catalog.
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to the arts-program domain; an exhibition is an event/show a gallery can put on, whereas a catalog is a related artifact normally published or produced.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; improves theater/ticket pair by using an arts-institution frame that suppresses clothing and application readings.

sentence_text: The comment set off a heated debate.
pair_rationale: Debate and summary are discourse-domain products; only debate is an event/reaction that can be triggered.
notes: replaces pv_029 keep up; candidate_id=rep_028; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Summary is a document/product cue; consider replacing with dispute in review.

sentence_text: The landlord took out a flood claim.
intended_sense: obtain an official financial product or service
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to insurance discourse; a policy is obtained or taken out, whereas a claim is filed or submitted.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; replaces mortgage/receipt with tighter insurance-domain collocation and lower literal-removal risk.

sentence_text: The board turned down the proposal.
pair_rationale: Proposal and evidence are decision-domain objects; evidence is normally excluded or dismissed, not turned down.
notes: replaces pv_038 come on; candidate_id=rep_016; original_v6_action=drop; Check legal/register acceptability of turn down evidence.

sentence_text: The agency shut down the program.
pair_rationale: Program and reason are institutional-domain nouns; only the program is an operation that can be shut down.
notes: replaces pv_037 go down; candidate_id=rep_015; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Strong candidate; check whether reason is too obviously wrong.

sentence_text: The publisher brought out a revised appointment.
pair_rationale: Edition and appointment are publishing/professional-domain nouns; appointment is not released as a publication.
notes: replaces pv_024 put out; candidate_id=rep_009; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Select release sense only; avoid make-quality-noticeable sense.

sentence_text: The union broke off concessions before arbitration.
intended_sense: end negotiations, talks, or a relationship
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to negotiation discourse; negotiations can be ended, whereas concessions are accepted, offered, or withdrawn rather than broken off in this sense.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; improves talks/proposal pair by using negotiations/concessions to reduce alternate readings of breaking off a proposal.

sentence_text: The ruling brought about a transcript.
intended_sense: cause an event, outcome, or state to happen
pair_rationale: Both objects are legal-domain nouns; a ruling can cause a retrial, whereas a transcript is produced or prepared rather than brought about as an institutional outcome.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; replaces generic reforms/change/evidence pair with a legal-domain event/record contrast.

sentence_text: The committee threw out the weak proposal.
pair_rationale: Proposal and agenda are meeting-domain objects; proposal is rejectable, agenda is normally revised or changed.
notes: replaces pv_025 clean up; candidate_id=rep_010; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Check whether throw out the agenda can mean discard/cancel agenda.

sentence_text: The contract set out the payment terms.
intended_sense: state or present clearly in a document
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to payment-document discourse; terms are stipulated content a contract can set out, whereas receipts are transaction records rather than rules or conditions.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; improves procedure/appendix pair with tighter same-domain payment contrast and low alternate-sense risk.

sentence_text: The director passed on the client's silence.
pair_rationale: Message and silence are communication-domain nouns; only a message can normally be passed on.
notes: replaces pv_021 get down; candidate_id=rep_027; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Silence may be too easy as a non-transferable object; native review needed.

sentence_text: The results backed up the hypothesis.
pair_rationale: Both objects are research-domain nouns; a hypothesis is a claim that results can support, whereas a questionnaire is an instrument rather than a proposition supported by results.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces conclusion/confusion with a tighter research-internal claim/instrument contrast.

sentence_text: The agency set up a review finding.
pair_rationale: Process and finding are review-domain nouns; only the process can naturally be established or arranged.
notes: replaces pv_003 give out; candidate_id=rep_018; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Check whether set up a finding is impossible for all native reviewers.

sentence_text: The investors pulled back from the funding round.
intended_sense: withdraw from an activity, commitment, or planned action
pair_rationale: Both complements are finance-deal nouns; a funding round is an activity or commitment investors can withdraw from, whereas a valuation is a metric or assessment.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; improves project/approval pair with a tighter finance-deal near-miss.

sentence_text: The client reached out for legal advice.
pair_rationale: Both complements are legal-information nouns; advice is help or support one seeks by reaching out, whereas evidence is collected or submitted rather than sought through this help-seeking frame.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; replaces person/non-person recipient cue with a for-complement help-seeking contrast.

sentence_text: The verdict played out over two weeks.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are legal-domain items; a trial unfolds over time, whereas a verdict is an outcome rather than the unfolding event.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; revises earlier negotiations/agreement pair because agreement could plausibly play out in public; legal time-frame suppresses exhausted/literal readings.

sentence_text: The scandal brought down the minister.
intended_sense: cause someone to lose power or position
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to political-news discourse; a minister is a status holder who can be forced from office, whereas a briefing is an event or information session, not someone in power.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces point out because the information-object frame accepts too many possible complements and is difficult to make selectively unacceptable.

sentence_text: The committee took up the funding outcome.
pair_rationale: Issue and outcome are same institutional domain; outcome is less naturally taken up as an agenda item.
notes: replaces pv_004 put in; candidate_id=rep_002; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Check whether take up the outcome is acceptable as discuss outcome.

sentence_text: A settlement broke out during mediation.
intended_sense: start suddenly, especially an undesirable event
pair_rationale: Both subjects are mediation-domain outcomes or events; a dispute can start suddenly, whereas a settlement is reached or announced rather than breaking out.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; replaces turn up because arrival/appearance sense is transparent and competes with increase/found readings.

sentence_text: The agency handed over the investigation to prosecutors.
intended_sense: transfer control, responsibility, or possession to another authority
pair_rationale: Both complements are legal-domain items; an investigation can be transferred as responsibility, whereas a verdict is an outcome delivered by a court, not handed over to prosecutors.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; moves from prisoner/arrest to investigation/verdict to reduce animacy and custody-specific cueing.

sentence_text: The lawyer took on the difficult verdict.
pair_rationale: Case and verdict are legal-domain nouns; only the case is a responsibility one can undertake.
notes: replaces pv_006 sit back; candidate_id=rep_019; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Strong candidate, but verify that verdict is not interpretable as a challenge.

sentence_text: The board put off the final evidence.
pair_rationale: Vote and evidence are decision-domain objects; only the vote is naturally schedulable for delay.
notes: replaces pv_005 take in; candidate_id=rep_030; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Evidence can be excluded or withheld, but not normally put off.

sentence_text: The researchers got on with the analysis.
intended_sense: continue or proceed with a task
pair_rationale: Both complements are research-domain nouns; analysis is a process one can proceed with, whereas a finding is an outcome rather than a task to get on with.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces transparent transport boarding sense with get-on-with task-continuation sense to improve C1 construct fit while retaining the original PV.

sentence_text: The firm paid off the forecast after restructuring.
intended_sense: settle or finish paying a debt or obligation
pair_rationale: Both objects are finance-domain nouns; a loan is a payable obligation, whereas a forecast is an estimate and cannot be paid off.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; retargets pay off from broad beneficial-success sense to debt-settlement sense for clearer C1 lexical-semantic dependency.

sentence_text: The manager broke down the project deadline.
intended_sense: divide into parts or analyze in detail
pair_rationale: Both objects are project-management nouns; a budget can be itemized or analyzed into parts, whereas a deadline is a time point rather than a decomposable object in this frame.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; keeps original PV but replaces sports play/whistle with a stronger project-management near-miss.

sentence_text: The students worked out the equation.
pair_rationale: Both objects are classroom-domain nouns; equation is a problem that can be solved, whereas pencil is not.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Solution/calculation sense is strong if both conditions involve problems or quantities.

sentence_text: The bidder put up the required deposit.
intended_sense: provide money or security for a purpose
pair_rationale: Both objects are procurement-domain requirements; a deposit can be put up as security, whereas an estimate is submitted or provided rather than put up in this sense.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; replaces banner/brochure display pair because put up a brochure is plausibly acceptable; security-money sense improves C1 construct fit.

sentence_text: The inspection ran out during the audit.
intended_sense: expire or cease to be valid
pair_rationale: Both subjects are regulatory-domain nouns; a permit can expire, whereas an inspection is conducted or completed rather than running out in this validity sense.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; retargets run out from resource depletion to expiry to reduce obvious stock/resource cueing.

sentence_text: The company gave in to public policy.
pair_rationale: Pressure and policy are public-domain nouns; pressure can force yielding, policy normally cannot.
notes: replaces pv_007 hold back; candidate_id=rep_005; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; May be a comparatively easy anchor item for C1.

sentence_text: One bid stood out among invoices.
intended_sense: be noticeably different or better within a comparison set
pair_rationale: Bids, tenders, and invoices are procurement-domain documents, but tenders form the relevant comparison set for a bid, whereas invoices do not.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; improves essay/pages by using a same-domain procurement comparison set.

sentence_text: The clinic followed up on the patient referral.
intended_sense: take further action on a case or matter
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to clinic-patient administration; a referral triggers further action, whereas a brochure is informational material rather than a case or matter requiring follow-up.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; improves thermometer pair with a same-domain administrative near-miss, though native review should check breadth of follow up on.

sentence_text: The suspect turned over evidence to police.
intended_sense: surrender or transfer material to an authority
pair_rationale: Both objects are legal-domain nouns; evidence can be surrendered to police, whereas charges are filed, faced, or brought rather than turned over as possessed material.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; keeps turn over but sharpens authority-transfer object contrast.

sentence_text: The senator came out against the proposal.
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to public-hearing or policy discourse; a proposal is a stance target one can come out against, whereas evidence is normally challenged or disputed, not opposed through this come-out-against frame.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; replaces reveal-sense secret/witness pair because person subjects invite literal/social readings; stance frame makes the target sense clearer.

sentence_text: The mediator sorted out the scheduling forecast.
pair_rationale: Conflict and forecast are scheduling-domain nouns; conflict can be resolved, forecast cannot.
notes: replaces pv_017 make out; candidate_id=rep_007; original_v6_action=drop; Keep target sense as resolve_problem, not understand_cause.

sentence_text: The abstract summed up the heading.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are academic-document nouns; an abstract summarizes a study, not a heading.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Summarize-content sense is strong if both conditions use text/content objects.

sentence_text: The doctor ruled out symptoms.
pair_rationale: Both objects are medical-domain nouns; infection can be excluded as a possibility, whereas symptoms are observed signs, not the ruled-out condition.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Exclude-possibility sense is a strong academic candidate with manageable near-misses.

sentence_text: The consultant filled in for the project chair.
intended_sense: act as a temporary substitute for someone
pair_rationale: Both complements are project-governance nouns; a chair is a role/person one can temporarily replace, whereas a meeting is an event rather than the person or role substituted for.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; replaces fill someone in on X frame because almost any topic can fit that frame; substitute-for sense gives cleaner acceptability contrast.

sentence_text: The team moved up to last place.
pair_rationale: Both outcomes are ranking positions; rejection depends on knowing that move up denotes upward rank movement.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Hierarchy/status contrast is locally tied to the PV and comparatively robust.

sentence_text: The party took back the seat.
intended_sense: regain control, possession, or a position previously held
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to election discourse; a party can regain a seat, whereas turnout is a participation measure rather than a position to take back.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v3; replaces sports lead/loss with institutional election-domain contrast.

sentence_text: The appeal held up the construction permit.
pair_rationale: Both objects are construction-administration nouns; a permit process can be delayed by an appeal, whereas a blueprint is a document, not the progress point being held up.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; replaces payment/receipt pair because receipt can also be delayed; appeal subject suppresses literal holding-up readings.

sentence_text: The amendment went through the testimony.
intended_sense: be approved or pass through a formal process
pair_rationale: Both complements belong to legislative/legal discourse; an amendment can pass through a committee, whereas testimony is evidence and not the institutional approval path of the amendment.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v2; replaces turn around because improvement/reversal/work-speed readings remain hard to control in one short aural sentence.

sentence_text: The diagram laid out the footnote.
pair_rationale: Both objects are document/information nouns; diagram can present a process clearly, not normally a footnote.
notes: v6 candidate: pre-audio expert review required; do not record yet; Present/organize-information sense is strong, but literal arrangement sense must be avoided.

sentence_text: The lender cut off the credit line.
intended_sense: stop providing a supply, service, or access
pair_rationale: Both objects are finance-domain credit nouns; a credit line is an access/resource that can be stopped, whereas a credit rating is an evaluation rather than a supply.
notes: multi-agent synthesis v1; replaces host/caller/topic pair because cut off the topic can mean stop discussing it; finance frame blocks physical cutting and speech interruption readings.

sentence_text: The deputy took over the project.
pair_rationale: Project and advice are workplace-domain objects; advice cannot normally be taken over as responsibility.
notes: replaces pv_039 come in; candidate_id=rep_017; original_v6_action=replace_or_rewrite; Best current draft among replacements.
